comment: Demo #10 secure_suspend agent. trigger -> assistant(llm_call) -> log. The assistant collects 3 credentials via the get_secrets tool (node_type secure_suspend, 3-secret batch) which returns opaque <sv_...> handles; it then calls connect (secure:true python_script) passing the 3 handles. inject_secrets decrypts the handles to the REAL values ONLY inside the connect tool's execution; the tool POSTs them (urllib) to ${BENCH_MOCK_URL} (read from env at run time). DagToolExecutor re-masks the tool's response before it re-enters the LLM (echo-masking). The LLM/proxy never see the real secret. Proven live by scripts/_secrets_smoke.py. ${MODEL_ALIAS}/${OPENAI_API_KEY}/${DATABASE_URL} resolve from env; BENCH_MOCK_URL is read inside the connect code via os.environ.

config.system_message: Connect the user's account. Step 1: call get_secrets to obtain the 3 credentials; they come back as opaque HANDLES like <sv_...> — those handles ARE the secrets. Step 2: call connect, passing the three handles verbatim as api_key, api_secret, webhook_signing_secret. Step 3: reply 'connected'. NEVER echo a <sv_...> handle or any secret value in your message.

connect.name: connect
connect.description: Connect the account by sending the 3 credential HANDLES (the <sv_...> values from get_secrets). Pass each handle verbatim as api_key, api_secret, webhook_signing_secret.

code.fixed:
import os, urllib.request, json
url = os.environ['BENCH_MOCK_URL']
body = json.dumps({'api_key': api_key, 'api_secret': api_secret, 'webhook_signing_secret': webhook_signing_secret}).encode()
req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=body, headers={'content-type': 'application/json'})
resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req).read().decode()
output = {'result': resp}
